description: Pilot benchmark for evaluating multimodal AI on well log display interpretation.
notes: Dolomite is a target class but has no labelled interval in this pilot. Descriptions were synthesised from petrophysical knowledge and visual interpretation of the log displays; expert geological validation is requested. Source log/interpretation PDFs are operator-provided and are NOT redistributed in this release; see DATASHEET.md.

intervals.description: High gamma ray readings above 100 GAPI indicating clay-rich formation. Mineral model shows dominant clay volume with magenta coloring. Low resistivity and high neutron porosity typical of shale.
intervals.visual_features: magenta fill, high GR deflection right, low resistivity

intervals.description: Continued high gamma ray response. Clay-dominated interval with characteristic shale properties. Density-neutron crossover present.
intervals.visual_features: magenta fill, hatched pattern, high GR

intervals.description: Sharp decrease in gamma ray to below 30 GAPI. Mineral model shows calcite-dominated composition with green coloring. High density around 2.71 g/cc consistent with limestone matrix.
intervals.visual_features: green fill, low GR deflection left, high density

intervals.description: Clean carbonate interval with low gamma ray. Calcite cement visible in mineral model. Moderate porosity indicated by density-neutron separation.
intervals.visual_features: green fill dominant, clean GR baseline

intervals.description: Limestone with minor clay content. Gamma ray slightly elevated but still in carbonate range. Good porosity development.
intervals.visual_features: green fill with minor magenta, moderate GR

intervals.description: Pure limestone interval. Very low gamma ray and high density. Mineral model shows nearly pure calcite composition.
intervals.visual_features: solid green fill, very low GR, high density track

intervals.description: Transition zone at base of carbonate sequence. Limestone with increasing siliciclastic content toward base.
intervals.visual_features: green fill transitioning, GR slightly increasing

intervals.description: Quartz-rich sandstone with low gamma ray. Mineral model shows yellow coloring indicating sand/quartz dominance. Good porosity.
intervals.visual_features: yellow fill, low GR, cyan water saturation

intervals.description: Clean sandstone reservoir interval. Very low gamma ray below 20 GAPI. High porosity visible in density-neutron overlay. Potential hydrocarbon zone.
intervals.visual_features: yellow fill dominant, very low GR, high porosity indication

intervals.description: Continued sand development with excellent reservoir properties. Uniform low gamma ray signature throughout interval.
intervals.visual_features: yellow fill, consistent low GR, good porosity

intervals.description: Return to shale facies. Gamma ray increases sharply above 80 GAPI. Clay volume dominates mineral model. Base of reservoir section.
intervals.visual_features: magenta fill returns, GR spike right, low resistivity
